name: causal_intervention_questions_v1
description: Clinical question stems for causal intervention benchmark

intervention.name: Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI / Angioplasty)
intervention.description: Catheter-based procedure to open blocked coronary arteries

intervention.name: Observation Only (No Intervention)
intervention.description: Patient monitored without procedural intervention

questions.question_stem:
You are a clinical AI evaluating trajectory predictions after medical interventions.

## Case A: Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI / Angioplasty)
**Patient:** 69-year-old Female
**Diagnoses:** Not listed
**Current Medications:** Not listed
**Intervention:** Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI / Angioplasty) - Catheter-based procedure to open blocked coronary arteries

**Lab Timeline (last 48 hours):**
No lab data available

Baseline Troponin I: 0.000 ng/mL

---

## Case B: Observation Only (No Intervention)
**Patient:** 42-year-old Female
**Diagnoses:** 008.45, 038.9, 041.04
**Current Medications:** Not listed
**Intervention:** Observation Only (No Intervention) - Patient monitored without procedural intervention

**Lab Timeline (last 48 hours):**
No lab data available

Baseline Troponin I: 0.000 ng/mL

---

## Question

Both patients have similar baseline conditions and lab trajectories leading up to these different interventions.

**Predict:** Will Case A's troponin trajectory differ from Case B's over the next 48 hours?

**Specifically:**
1. Which patient's troponin will change more (fall/rise/stay stable)?
2. Approximately how much change do you expect in each case?
3. When (how many hours) will you expect to see the intervention effect?

Provide your reasoning based on the intervention type and expected physiological effects.

direction_accuracy.description: Did model predict the correct direction of change for each case?

causal_comparison.description: Did model correctly predict which case would change more?
criteria: Correctly identified which trajectory is larger

timing_estimate.description: Did model estimate reasonable timing for intervention effect?
criteria: For pci: expected within typical range
For observation: expected within typical range

metadata.expected_effect_a: Troponin should fall as myocardial damage is limited
metadata.expected_effect_b: Depends on natural course of disease
